nodes.name: ruvix
nodes.summary: RuVix Cognition Kernel: capability-based microhypervisor for AI agents with 12 syscalls, 6 primitives, proof-gated mutation, and no_std support; Cargo workspace with 24 member crates plus tests/benches/examples.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Workspace manifest declaring 24 ruvix-* member crates (types, region, queue, cap, proof, sched, boot, vecgraph, nucleus, hal, aarch64, drivers, smp, physmem, dma, dtb, net, fs, bcm2711, rpi-boot, shell, cli) plus qemu-swarm, tests, benches, and cognitive_demo example.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Top-level RuVix documentation: motivation vs Linux/seL4/Zephyr, six kernel primitives, twelve syscalls, three proof tiers (Reflex <10us, Standard ~100us, Deep ~1ms), crate overview across Phases A-E, and ADR-087 cross-reference.

nodes.name: config.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo build configuration pinning the custom aarch64-ruvix.json target with rust-lld linker, QEMU virt+cortex-a72 runner, and unstable build-std for core/alloc with compiler-builtins-mem.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: AArch64 bare-metal boot infrastructure docs: memory layout (0x40000000 base, 128MiB RAM, 64KiB stack), linker.ld sections, QEMU virt memory map, build/run/debug Make targets, and Rust target spec details.

nodes.name: build.rs
nodes.summary: Cargo build script that re-runs on linker.ld changes and injects -Tlinker.ld as a rustc link argument.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Build-script entry point emitting cargo:rerun-if-changed and rustc-link-arg directives for the custom linker script.

tour.title: What is ruvix? The Microhypervisor at a Glance
tour.description: Start with the top-level RuVix workspace: a no_std, capability-based Rust microhypervisor with 17 syscalls, six kernel primitives, three proof tiers, and 26 sub-crates. The README explains motivation versus Linux/seL4/Zephyr, and the workspace Cargo.toml enumerates every member crate you'll meet later in the tour.

tour.title: Typed Handles and Kernel ABI
tour.description: Before any subsystem can talk to another, every kernel object needs a stable identity. ruvix-types defines the 64-bit Handle (id+generation), the ObjectType discriminator, the KernelError ABI, and the Capability/Proof/Region/Task handles every other crate imports — making this the single most fan-in-heavy file in the slice.

tour.title: Hardware Abstraction Layer
tour.description: ruvix-hal defines the architecture-agnostic seams the kernel depends on: Console, InterruptController, Mmu, Timer, and PowerManagement traits. Concrete drivers in later steps implement these traits, so the kernel never names a CPU architecture directly.

tour.title: ARM64 Implementation & BCM2711 Drivers
tour.description: These two crates land the HAL traits on real silicon. ruvix-aarch64 installs the EL1 vector base, configures SCTLR/TCR/MAIR, and provides exception handlers; ruvix-bcm2711 supplies Raspberry Pi 4 peripherals — Mini UART console, mailbox, GPIO, and the BCM interrupt controller — built on volatile MMIO primitives.

tour.title: Pi Boot & Device Tree Discovery
tour.description: Before the kernel starts, rpi-boot drops EL2->EL1, configures the MMU/TLB, parks secondary CPUs in the spin table, and parses the kernel command line. The ruvix-dtb crate then walks the Flattened Device Tree (zero-allocation) to discover hardware topology that the boot pipeline turns into capabilities.

tour.title: The 5-Stage Boot Pipeline
tour.description: ruvix-boot orchestrates the canonical bring-up: Hardware probe -> Verify ML-DSA-65 signature -> Create tasks -> Mount RVF regions -> Attest. BootLoader drives the stages and emits a BootResult containing root capabilities, an attestation chain (rolling SHA-256), and an append-only witness log — establishing the trusted base every later step relies on.

tour.title: The Capability System
tour.description: ruvix-cap implements the security spine: a CapabilityManager grants/revokes Handle-tagged tokens, a derivation tree enables cascading revocation, and Grant validation enforces rights monotonicity and delegation depth. Boot.rs seeds the root task's initial capability set per ADR-087 §6.3; the audit module periodically scans for orphans and over-broad rights.

tour.title: Proof Engine & Tier Router
tour.description: ruvix-proof gates every mutation behind a tiered proof: Reflex (<10us), Standard (~100us), or Deep (~1ms). The ProofEngine generates tokens, the tier router selects a tier per mutation context, the ProofVerifier checks capabilities and caches results with TTL+nonce uniqueness, and witness.rs produces Merkle inclusion proofs that flow back into the boot witness log.

tour.title: Memory: Physmem, Regions, DMA
tour.description: Three crates compose the memory story. ruvix-physmem is a buddy allocator over physical frames; ruvix-region adds policy-gated virtual regions (Immutable / AppendOnly / Slab) on top of pluggable MemoryBacking implementations; ruvix-dma exposes a platform-agnostic DmaController trait with channels, descriptors, and cache-coherent buffers for high-throughput device transfers.

tour.title: Scheduler: EDF + Novelty + Risk
tour.description: ruvix-sched is the coherence-aware scheduler from ADR-087 §5. PartitionManager round-robins between partitions with time slices; within a partition the priority formula combines EDF deadline urgency, a centroid-based novelty boost (EMA-updated), and a structural-risk penalty. Task control blocks track Ready/Running/Blocked/Terminated lifecycle plus the coherence delta the proof engine consumes.

tour.title: Queues, FS, and Network — Kernel Services
tour.description: Three service crates sit above the memory and scheduler layers. ruvix-queue provides lock-free SPSC RingBuffers wrapped in KernelQueues for inter-task IPC; ruvix-fs exposes a VFS with FAT32 + RamFs over a BlockDevice trait; ruvix-net is a minimal Ethernet/ARP/IPv4/UDP/ICMP stack composed over a NetworkDevice trait.

tour.title: VecGraph: Kernel-Resident Cognition Store
tour.description: ruvix-vecgraph is what makes ruvix a *cognition* kernel: a vector store with SIMD distance kernels (NEON/AVX2/scalar) and an HNSW index, plus a graph store of nodes and edges. Both stores enforce proof_policy on every mutation, track per-vector coherence, and append signed attestations to a witness log that ties back to the proof engine from Step 8.

tour.title: SMP: Multi-Core Primitives
tour.description: ruvix-smp wires the kernel for up to 256 cores. CpuId/CpuState track per-CPU lifecycle on an atomic state machine; PerCpu<T> with CacheAligned wrappers avoid false sharing; ticket-based fair spinlocks provide FIFO mutual exclusion; and the IPI layer dispatches IpiMessages across GICv3 with stats. Barriers wrap DMB/DSB/ISB/SEV for portable ordering.

tour.title: Nucleus: The 17-Syscall Surface
tour.description: ruvix-nucleus is the integration crate: the Kernel object aggregates capabilities, regions, scheduler, vector/graph stores, witness log, and proof engine into one place. syscall.rs defines the ADR-087 17-syscall set (spawn / cap_grant / region_map / queue / timer / vector / graph / sensor / checkpoint / witness / ...), and Checkpoint+ReplayEngine give deterministic snapshot/replay.

tour.title: Operating ruvix: Shell, Host CLI, and the Cognitive Demo
tour.description: The capstone closes the loop. ruvix-shell exposes a 13-command in-kernel debug shell via the ShellBackend trait (the most fan-in-heavy trait in the slice). The host ruvix-cli drives the device — build / flash / monitor / dtb / keys / security. The cognitive_demo example wires SensorAdapter -> FeatureExtractor -> ReasoningEngine -> Attestor -> Coordinator over queues, exercising proof-gated VecGraph mutations end-to-end, and the integration-tests crate validates ADR-087 §17 acceptance.
